questions.scenario: Users need to reach `orders.devpolaris.com`, but the API should reach Azure SQL over a private address. Which design matches that requirement?

options.text: Public entry point for users, private endpoint and private DNS for SQL.
Public SQL endpoint so the browser and API use the same path.
Private endpoint for the browser traffic and public endpoint for SQL.
No DNS records, because private access removes name resolution.

questions.explanation: Public user traffic and private data access are different paths. The app can be public-facing while the database stays private.

questions.scenario: A private endpoint exists for Azure SQL, but the app still resolves the public SQL hostname. What is likely missing or wrong?

options.text: Private DNS integration or the private DNS A record.

questions.explanation: A private endpoint gives the resource a private IP. Private DNS is what makes the normal service name resolve to that private IP.

questions.scenario: Which resources are reasonable public entry resources for `devpolaris-orders-api`?

options.text: Front Door for a global HTTPS entry.
Application Gateway for regional HTTP routing.

questions.explanation: The public path can include DNS and an HTTP entry point. A private endpoint for SQL should not become the public user door.
